change: you change the WBFT extra-data encoding/layout (WBFTExtra struct fields / RLP order)

impact.why: Encode counterpart: serializes the WBFTExtra field list in fixed order; must match any layout/field change.
Decode counterpart: anonymous struct field order + rlp:"nil" tags must mirror the encoded layout exactly.
WBFTExtra.EpochInfo is a nested RLP element; changing the epoch sub-layout changes the extra-data layout.
Nested under EpochInfo.Candidates; part of the same RLP layout contract.
Canonical reader: rlp.DecodeBytes(h.Extra) into WBFTExtra; every header consumer funnels through it.
Mutates and re-encodes WBFTExtra back into header.Extra during sealing/finalize; round-trips the layout.
Constructs a zero-value WBFTExtra and rlp.EncodeToBytes; field set must track the struct.
~9 sites (verifyHeader, seal, epoch, gasTip) read decoded WBFTExtra fields; semantics shift if fields move.
Parent/header validation reads WBFTExtra seals; affected by layout/field changes.
RPC API surfaces decoded WBFTExtra; output/contract changes with layout.
Header consumer that decodes WBFTExtra to read GasTip; breaks if GasTip position/presence changes.
Genesis producer: calls CreateInitialExtraData to set genesis.ExtraData; the produced bytes must match the new layout.
Test/chain-maker genesis producer also emits encoded WBFTExtra via CreateInitialExtraData.
Builds a &types.WBFTExtra literal and encodes it for the simulated backend genesis; field set must match.

grep_would_miss: Header.GasTip in core/types/block.go — grep for 'CreateInitialExtraData' or 'WBFTExtra{' misses it; it only calls ExtractWBFTExtra.
The ~9 engine.go ExtractWBFTExtra read sites that depend on field positions — a grep for the seed name finds none of them.
EpochInfo/Candidate nested RLP codecs — coupled by RLP nesting, not by textual reference to WBFTExtra.
notes: Seed CreateInitialExtraData (config.go:227) excluded as the definition. The true layout contract lives in istanbul.go Encode/DecodeRLP; CreateInitialExtraData is just one producer. cks find_callers/impact_analysis returned empty edges for these symbols, so the set was built from ripgrep (ExtractWBFTExtra callers + WBFTExtra producers) confirmed by Read.
